reviews.author: Vic
reviews.text: We discovered Lan Ha Bay, a less crowded alternative to Ha Long Bay, offering a serene escape for couples. The majestic limestone karsts rising from emerald waters create a breathtaking backdrop. We enjoyed relaxing, swimming in secluded lagoons and exploring hidden caves. The area's colorful culture is evident in the floating villages, where we observed local life. With fewer tourists, Lan Ha Bay provides a more intimate experience of Vietnam's natural wonders. It's an ideal destination for those seeking tranquility and unspoiled beauty in a romantic setting. We paid the tour 650k from our Hotel: The Wave View Hotel. Tour starts 8.30am and finishes at 5pm. Lunch included, plus kayaking, swimming in caves, monkey islands, etc It was great, I'd recommend it!

reviews.author: R Lim
reviews.text: Lovely bay for kayaking and swimming. Less touristy than Ha Long Bay and definitely not so crowded. Lovely sunset. Highly recommended to visit ☺️

reviews.author: Warshi Dandeniya
reviews.text: A wonderful place and a MUST visit if you are touring Vietnam. The Bay area is well organized but not much of a wonder… But if you take the cruise to go around towering islands and spend one night at least on water you will have a wonderful experience worthy for a lifetime. I really enjoyed it. We joined BlueDiamond Cruise. Their hospitality was great and the facilities and activities were very nice. I’m really happy that I joined this trip. Will definitely go there with my family once again. I saw many families with small kids and disabled persons visiting the area taking cruise ships.

reviews.author: Duy Cường Nguyễn
reviews.text: Personal impression is that Lan Ha Bay is more beautiful than Halong Bay. The bay is located approximately 30 km from the city center. It is situated to the east of Cat Ba Island and serves as a connecting link between Cat Ba Island and Halong Bay. From the ferry terminal on Cat Ba Island, it takes about 30 minutes to reach Lan Ha Bay by boat. The bay spans an area of over 7,000 hectares, with more than 5,400 hectares falling under the management of the UNESCO-recognized Cat Ba Biosphere Reserve - Cat Ba National Park. Lan Ha Bay has a curved shape, surrounded by over 400 different small and large islands. The name "Lan Ha" means "orchid" and symbolizes the beauty bestowed upon the world. While it may not have as many islands as Halong Bay, Lan Ha Bay stands out with its 139 small sandy beaches such as Ang Vem, Cat Dua, Cat Co, Ben Beo, Co Tien, Van Boi, and Van Ha. Lan Ha-Cat Ba Bay is located in the northern tourism region, so the weather here experiences all four seasons: spring, summer, autumn, and winter. Each season has its own unique and poetic beauty. Depending on personal preferences and schedules, visitors can plan their time to explore this place accordingly.

reviews.author: Pratik Gadgil
reviews.text: A few nautical miles away from the Cat Ba Island, lies the wonderful bay yet to be fully explored, and less crowded, and to a large extent can be called an alternative to the world famous Ha Long Bay. Half-day and full-day packages starting from VND 500,000 are available from Cat Ba Island, and there is a very small beach inside where one can spend an hour or so apart from enjoying the breathtaking views of the bay. One must take a group tour to go here, and it will include the cost of permit. As one passes from the water, one gets to see the sui generis rock formations, and as one goes deeper the hidden caves start coming into the picture. The caves are generally small and need a small boat to get through, and ideally the best way is kayaking, and it's worth the try. Sometimes the water gets shallow while traversing through these caves, and the width becomes narrow with rocks on the way such that there comes a possibility of banging the kayak on the rocks ( although it won't hurt at all ). The waters at times seem to have a bit of litter, and upon inquiring I was told that it comes from Ha Long Bay. If you go further and further, you enter the Ha Long Bay, but a full tour of the same cannot happen in a day from Cat Ba Island.

reviews.author: Ho Ngoc Tin
reviews.text: Amazing island. We have book a tour from Kitetravel with 15$/person. The tour starts from 8:30am and finish at 4:30pm. The first go to Monkey island and hike to the top for great view. After that go around Lan Ha bay and try kayak and having lunch. Unbelievable price! The tour guide is good English communication and supportive. No more cost required. This tour is a half price compare with Ha Long tour, but the view is more amazing.

reviews.author: Anh Tung Vu
reviews.text: Beautiful sightseeing! Glad they still keep it clean and natural. Take a boat, go around, take some nice pictures, watch the local fishing village, kayaking and swimming under the sunshine. Recommended to go on winter to fully enjoy the bay in peace.

reviews.author: Nhuy Nguyen
reviews.text: A peaceful weekend at Lan ha bay. There’s a direct highest cruise from cat ba to hai phong. Else you can take transfer bus to ben pha vieng and then take highest cruise to hai phong. Ticket 140k vnd

reviews.author: Hang Nguyen
reviews.text: Amazing natural landscapes, must visit if you have chance to Hai Phong. We enjoyed our 1day tour in Lan Ha Bay and 2 days in Cat Ba. Our small family did Kayaking and swimming on the Bay, my little 3yo boy loved this place so much and we would love to come back.

reviews.author: KN
reviews.text: Le Trung - Cat Ba View said: This is a must see place. So i had very much fun on this .

reviews.author: Nam Pham
reviews.text: Traveling Lan Ha Bay is "a forgotten paradise" in Vietnam Located in the south of Ha Long Bay and in the east of Cat Ba Island, Lan Ha Bay has an area of over 7,000 hectares with outstanding beauty of about 400 large and small islands, with many interesting shapes. Unlike Ha Long Bay, all islands in Lan Ha Bay are covered with trees, even small islands. With a cool and fresh climate, visitors can travel to Lan Ha Bay at any time of the year. However, domestic tourists often travel to Lan Ha Bay from April to October, while international visitors often visit Lan Ha from November to March.

reviews.author: Vitalina Kovalenko
reviews.text: I visited Lan Ha bay a few days ago and can confirm that it no longer exists. A huge construction company is building a huge hotel here. Therefore no bay left. Construction sounds all day long. I am confused by many positive RECENT reviews left here. I have a suspicion that they are fake and might be written by persons affiliated to that construction company to cover up other negative reviews you can find below which state likewise that lan ha bay no longer exists. I am texting my theory to see if some positive review will be left after mine by some “Mr Dongs”

reviews.author: Chloe Kelly
reviews.text: This is no longer bay!! They have built over the water and is currently (september 2025) a building site, to be a resort. Very disappointing, no bay, lack of care for the locals and their businesses

reviews.author: OFFICIAL Viclaniana
reviews.text: A wonderful place for holidays , i came here when the weather was quite hot, it caused my skin to burn quickly, but this was a great adventure (sea is like a painting). Although some places litter the trash.

reviews.author: Bao Tran
reviews.text: Very beautiful places, recommend visiting when come Vietnam.

reviews.author: Helen Kiyoko
reviews.text: Great experience! I love the beauty of the nature here. Highly recommend for everyone who visit Cat Ba!

reviews.author: Tobias Trong
reviews.text: Lan Hạ Bay -- A great destination for those avoiding the bustle and hustle of Hạ Long Bay

reviews.author: Mark Vanderwee
reviews.text: Beautiful view from Cat Ba beach 1 and2

reviews.author: Bee Ads
reviews.text: Don't have anything better to say. The pictures do itself well

reviews.author: Maren Sander
reviews.text: Not too many tourists, but a lot of waste is swimming in the sea.

reviews.author: Maximilian Linner
reviews.text: NOT existing anymore! Construction site. Big resorts are coming

reviews.author: karmela ban
reviews.text: Beautifull bay just next to Halong Bay. Breathtaking place. Just love it.

reviews.author: andy maphrao
reviews.text: now in 2025 it's all destroyed by a giant real estate project all the sea front hotels and restaurants become to be at the back side of it

reviews.author: Felicia
reviews.text: very quiet at night, kayaking here is awesome

reviews.author: Tarun Singh
reviews.text: Fantastic location and a great view. There are not many cruises in the area. Great place for kayaking and exploring the bay. The location is clean and not much garbage floating around. This location provides a good background for instagram worthy photos.

reviews.author: VijayMilan Math
reviews.text: Lan Ha Bay is truly a beauty. One of the most important tourist destinations of North Vietnam, this place has also many tales to tell . A local , or a tourist guide will be able to tell you many interesting titbits of information. February was a good time of the year to visit this bay, since it gets overly crowded during the summer.

reviews.author: Shishir Deshpande
reviews.text: Was an amazing experience with a reasonably detailed and elaborate boat ride of around 5-6 hours or so and a hotel pick up and a drop. Hours sailing through the pristine Ha Long Bay to the Lan Ha Bay. Not only were the boat staff efficient and courteous the food was reasonablely good too (seafood). Free kayaks was the part of the deal and that and the beauty of this place were the highlights of my Vietnam trip!!

reviews.author: Hai Binh Nguyen
reviews.text: Like halong bay but it smaller. Trash. Outside of it is quiet and beauty. Can you see me?
